blurb: Use multiplication and division to solve worded rate problems.

blurb: Estimate to judge the magnitude of a decimal multiplication.

blurb: Combine and find the difference of fractions sharing a denominator.

blurb: Apply same-denominator addition and subtraction to worded problems.

blurb: Solve duration problems in 12- and 24-hour notation.

blurb: Solve $ax^{2}+bx+c=0$ by factorising, including non-monic quadratics.

blurb: Apply the log laws to evaluate and simplify expressions.

blurb: Solve simple equations involving exponents or logarithms.

title: Determine an annuity contribution

title: Solve annuity problems
blurb: Solve annuity problems involving superannuation, savings accounts and loans.

title: Apply the sine rule for obtuse angles
blurb: Apply the sine rule to find an unknown angle that is obtuse.

title: Interpret compass radial surveys
blurb: Construct and interpret compass radial surveys, finding angles at the centre and the area and perimeter of the surveyed land.

title: Apply the capture–recapture technique
blurb: Solve practical ratio problems using the capture–recapture technique.

title: Calculate area from a scaled plan
blurb: Calculate perimeter or area from a site plan, survey or map, using the Trapezoidal rule where appropriate.

title: Calculate rainfall volume
blurb: Calculate the volume of rainfall over an area using $V = Ah$.

title: Examine fuel consumption rates
blurb: Calculate fuel consumption rates in km/L and L/100 km, and use them to find fuel used, distance travelled and fuel cost.

title: Calculate energy use in kilowatt hours
blurb: Convert between watts and kilowatts and calculate kilowatt hours and running costs.

title: Use network flow terminology
blurb: Define and use source, sink, inflow, outflow and cut.

title: Construct a flow network
blurb: Convert information in a table into a weighted, directed flow network.

title: Calculate maximum flow by saturating edges
blurb: Find the maximum flow of a network by listing source-to-sink paths, pushing each path's minimum remaining capacity through and subtracting until edges saturate, then summing the path flows.

title: Calculate the capacity of a cut
blurb: Identify whether a line is a valid cut (fully separating source from sink) and calculate its capacity, counting only edges that flow from the source side to the sink side.

title: Apply the maximum-flow minimum-cut theorem
blurb: Find the minimum cut of a network and apply the maximum-flow minimum-cut theorem, including verifying whether a given cut is minimal.

title: Analyse network flow capacity
blurb: Examine whether a network's flow capacity meets demand, and analyse the effect of increasing or reducing the capacity of an edge, using the minimum cut to decide which edge to change.

title: Construct a network from a precedence table
blurb: Construct a network diagram from a precedence table, including dummy activities.

title: Apply forward and backward scanning
blurb: Find the earliest and latest starting times using forward and backward scanning.

title: Calculate float times
blurb: Calculate float times to identify critical and non-critical activities.

title: Determine the critical path
blurb: Locate the critical path and determine the minimum completion time of a project.

title: Construct a Gantt chart
blurb: Construct a Gantt chart from a network diagram, indicating the critical path.

title: Crash a project to reduce completion time
blurb: Shorten a project's minimum completion time by reducing the duration of critical activities, finding the maximum useful reduction before a new critical path forms, and choosing the least-cost reductions.

title: Find the equation of a line of best fit
blurb: Determine and interpret the intercept and gradient of a line of best fit.

title: Make predictions using the regression equation
blurb: Substitute a value into a line-of-best-fit equation $y = a + bx$ to predict the other variable, deciding whether the given value is $x$ or $y$ and rearranging to solve when the dependent variable is given.

title: Calculate Pearson's correlation coefficient
blurb: Calculate and interpret Pearson's correlation coefficient r for a bivariate dataset.

title: Find the least-squares regression line
blurb: Determine the equation of the least-squares regression line using a calculator.

title: Calculate expected frequency
blurb: Calculate the expected frequency of an event as number of trials × probability ($f = np$).

title: Recognise a normal distribution
blurb: Recognise normally distributed data and its bell-shaped curve, with mean $\approx$ median $\approx$ mode.

title: Calculate a z-score
blurb: Calculate a $z$-score using $z = \dfrac{x - \mu}{\sigma}$.

title: Convert a z-score to a raw score
blurb: Convert a $z$-score back to a raw value with $x = \mu + z\sigma$, and rearrange to find the mean or standard deviation from a known score and $z$.

title: Compare values using z-scores
blurb: Use z-scores to compare scores from different datasets and justify conclusions.

title: Apply the empirical rule
blurb: Apply the 68–95–99.7 rule to normally distributed data.

title: Calculate normal probabilities
blurb: Calculate probabilities using z-scores and the empirical rule and shade areas under the curve.

title: Find normal probabilities from the standard normal table
blurb: Use the standard normal table of $P(z<c)$ to find normal probabilities — reading $P(z<c)$ directly, using $P(z>c) = 1 - P(z<c)$, subtracting for $P(a<z<b)$, and using symmetry for negative $z$ — then applying $f = np$ for expected counts.

title: Rationalise binomial surd denominators
blurb: Identify the conjugate and rationalise denominators of the form $\dfrac{a}{\sqrt{b}\pm\sqrt{c}}$ and $\dfrac{\sqrt{a}\pm\sqrt{d}}{\sqrt{b}\pm\sqrt{c}}$.

title: Find conditions on a parameter from the discriminant
blurb: Use $\Delta=b^{2}-4ac$ to find conditions for roots to be equal, distinct, real or rational, and find unknown coefficients.

title: Identify the zeroes of a function
blurb: Relate the $x$-intercepts of $y=f(x)$ to the zeroes and the solutions of $f(x)=0$.

title: Equate coefficients of equal quadratics
blurb: Use that two quadratic functions are equal for all x if and only if corresponding coefficients are equal.

title: Find the equation of a parabola from features
blurb: Determine the equation of a parabola given sufficient graphical features.

title: Solve quadratic inequalities
blurb: Solve quadratic inequalities using the graph or sign of the quadratic.

title: Describe the behaviour of a reciprocal function
blurb: Describe the behaviour of $f(x)=k/x$ as $x\to\infty$ and $x\to-\infty$.

title: Model direct variation $y=kx^{n}$
blurb: Develop models of the form $y=kx^{n}$ and find $k$ from a pair of values, then find other values.

title: Model inverse variation $y=k/x^{n}$
blurb: Develop models of the form $y=k/x^{n}$ and find $k$ from a pair of values, then find other values.

title: Solve direct and inverse variation problems
blurb: Analyse and solve problems involving direct and inverse variation.

title: Graph semicircles
blurb: Identify and graph the semicircles $y=\pm\sqrt{r^{2}-x^{2}}$ and $x=\pm\sqrt{r^{2}-y^{2}}$.

title: Graph the square root function
blurb: Graph $y=\sqrt{x}$ and its translations $y=\sqrt{x-h}+k$, stating the domain and range.

title: Express domain and range in interval notation
blurb: Determine and describe the domain and range of functions and relations using interval notation, inequalities or words.

title: Find the domain and range of a composite function
blurb: Find the domain and range of a composite function $f(g(x))$ by restricting the inner domain so that its outputs lie in the domain of the outer function.

title: Test for even and odd functions
blurb: Define even and odd functions and test using $f(-x)=f(x)$ and $f(-x)=-f(x)$.

title: Form and evaluate composite functions
blurb: Use the composite function f(g(x)), where the output of g becomes the input of f.

title: Determine equations of composite functions
blurb: Determine the equation of a composite function f(g(x)).

title: Interpret piecewise-defined functions
blurb: Interpret functions defined differently on different parts of the domain.

title: Graph piecewise-defined functions
blurb: Graph piecewise-defined functions and determine their domain, range and parity, marking open and closed endpoints.

title: Identify continuity and discontinuities
blurb: Define continuity informally and identify points where functions are not continuous.

title: Define the absolute value
blurb: Define |x| as the distance from the origin and use the piecewise definition.

title: Write an absolute value as a piecewise function
blurb: Write $\lvert ax+b\rvert$ (or $\lvert f(x)\rvert$) as a piecewise-defined function by splitting at the zero of the argument.

title: Use $\sqrt{x^{2}}=|x|$
blurb: Show using numerical substitution that $\sqrt{x^{2}}=|x|$ and use the result.

title: Graph absolute value functions
blurb: Graph $y=|x|$ and $y=|ax+b|$, identifying symmetry, domain and range.

title: Solve absolute value equations
blurb: Solve equations of the form $|ax+b|=k$ algebraically and graphically.

title: Define trig ratios for any magnitude
blurb: Define $\cos\theta=x/r$, $\sin\theta=y/r$ and $\tan\theta=y/x$ for a point on a circle of radius $r$ and extend to angles of any magnitude.

title: Evaluate trig ratios at multiples of $90^{\circ}$
blurb: Evaluate $\sin$, $\cos$ and $\tan$ at multiples of $90^{\circ}$ and identify where the ratios are undefined.

title: Use the related angle
blurb: Identify the related (reference) angle and use it to obtain trig ratios of an angle of any magnitude.

title: Determine the sign of a trig ratio using ASTC
blurb: Identify the quadrant of an angle and use ASTC to decide whether $\sin\theta$, $\cos\theta$ or $\tan\theta$ is positive or negative.

title: Find ratios for $180^{\circ}\pm A$ and $360^{\circ}-A$
blurb: Develop and use the trigonometric ratios for angles of the form $180^{\circ}\pm A$ and $360^{\circ}-A$ to find exact values.

title: Use negative-angle relationships
blurb: Use $\cos(-\theta)=\cos\theta$, $\sin(-\theta)=-\sin\theta$ and $\tan(-\theta)=-\tan\theta$.

title: Examine proofs of the triangle rules
blurb: Examine the proofs of the sine rule, the cosine rule and the area of a triangle formula.

title: Apply the ambiguous case of the sine rule
blurb: Examine and apply the ambiguous case of the sine rule, where two solutions for an angle are possible.

title: Define radian measure
blurb: Define a radian as arc length $\div$ radius and explain why $360^{\circ}=2\pi$ radians.

title: Convert between degrees and radians
blurb: Convert angle measures between degrees and radians.

title: Find exact ratios for multiples of $\tfrac{\pi}{6}$ and $\tfrac{\pi}{4}$
blurb: Find the exact sine, cosine and tangent for integer multiples of $\tfrac{\pi}{6}$ and $\tfrac{\pi}{4}$.

title: Graph trig functions in radians
blurb: Graph $y=\sin x$, $y=\cos x$ and $y=\tan x$ over domains in degrees or radians, showing intercepts, asymptotes, period, amplitude and parity.

title: Find arc length using $l=r\theta$
blurb: Use $l=r\theta$ for the length of an arc subtending angle $\theta$ (in radians) at the centre of a circle.

title: Find sector area using $A=\tfrac{1}{2}r^{2}\theta$
blurb: Use $A=\tfrac{1}{2}r^{2}\theta$ for the area of a sector with angle $\theta$ (in radians).

title: Solve arc, sector and segment problems
blurb: Solve problems involving arc lengths and the areas of major and minor sectors and segments.

title: Define sec, cosec and cot
blurb: Define $\sec\theta$, $\operatorname{cosec}\theta$ and $\cot\theta$ using right-triangle ratios and a circle of radius $r$.

title: Find exact sec, cosec and cot ratios
blurb: Find the exact secant, cosecant and cotangent ratios for $30^{\circ}$, $45^{\circ}$, $60^{\circ}$ and multiples of $\tfrac{\pi}{6}$ and $\tfrac{\pi}{4}$.

title: Use the reciprocal identities
blurb: Use $\sec A=\dfrac{1}{\cos A}$, $\operatorname{cosec} A=\dfrac{1}{\sin A}$ and $\cot A=\dfrac{1}{\tan A}$, identifying the excluded angles.

title: Use the quotient identities
blurb: Establish and use $\tan\theta=\dfrac{\sin\theta}{\cos\theta}$ and $\cot\theta=\dfrac{\cos\theta}{\sin\theta}$, identifying excluded angles.

title: Prove the complementary angle identities
blurb: Prove and use the complementary angle identities for all six trigonometric ratios.

title: Prove the Pythagorean identities
blurb: Prove and use $\cos^{2}x+\sin^{2}x=1$, $1+\tan^{2}x=\sec^{2}x$ and $1+\cot^{2}x=\operatorname{cosec}^{2}x$.

title: Evaluate trig expressions from one ratio
blurb: Evaluate trigonometric expressions given one ratio and the quadrant, using identities.

title: Solve trig equations on a restricted domain
blurb: Solve trigonometric equations in degrees or radians on a restricted domain.

title: Solve trig equations reducible to quadratics
blurb: Solve trigonometric equations that reduce to quadratic equations on a restricted domain.

title: Solve exp/log equations reducible to quadratics
blurb: Solve exponential and logarithmic equations that reduce to quadratics by substituting $u=a^{x}$ or $u=\log_a x$.

title: Solve trig equations with compound angles
blurb: Solve trigonometric equations whose argument is a compound angle such as $\sin 2x$ or $\cos(x+60^{\circ})$ by substituting for the compound angle, transforming the domain, solving, then back-substituting.

title: Simplify trigonometric expressions
blurb: Apply identities to simplify trig expressions, reducing to sin x and cos x.

title: Prove trigonometric identities
blurb: Prove trigonometric identities using substitution and reduction to sin x and cos x.

title: Calculate an average rate of change
blurb: Calculate the average rate of change of f(x) over [a,b] as the gradient of the secant.

title: Find average speed from a graph
blurb: Use the average rate of change to determine the average speed of an object from a distance–time graph.

title: Relate instantaneous rate to the tangent
blurb: Relate the instantaneous rate of change to the gradient of the tangent and distinguish it from the average rate.

title: Estimate an instantaneous rate from a graph
blurb: Estimate the instantaneous rate of change at a point by drawing a tangent on a graph.

title: Approximate gradient with the secant limit
blurb: Approximate the gradient of $f(x)=x^{n}$ at a point using $\dfrac{f(c+h)-f(c)}{h}$ as $h\to 0$.

title: Infer the derivative of $x^{n}$
blurb: Infer that $nx^{n-1}$ is the gradient function of $f(x)=x^{n}$.

title: Define the derivative
blurb: Define f'(x) as the gradient of the tangent and recognise it as the derivative or gradient function.

title: Differentiate from first principles
blurb: Use $f'(x)=\lim_{h\to 0}\dfrac{f(x+h)-f(x)}{h}$ to differentiate, including quadratic functions.

title: Differentiate constant and linear functions
blurb: Find the derivatives of constant and linear functions.

title: Apply the power rule
blurb: Apply $\dfrac{d}{dx}(x^{n})=nx^{n-1}$ for all real $n$, including negative and fractional indices.

title: Differentiate sums and multiples
blurb: Differentiate sums and constant multiples of power functions, including polynomials.

title: Find tangents and normals
blurb: Find the equations of tangents and normals to a curve at a given point.

title: Find points with a given tangent gradient
blurb: Find points on a curve where the tangent or normal has a given gradient.

title: Relate tangent gradient to inclination
blurb: Use $\tan\theta=m$ to relate the angle of inclination of a tangent to its gradient.

title: Apply the product rule
blurb: Apply the product rule to differentiate a product of two functions.

title: Apply the quotient rule
blurb: Apply the quotient rule to differentiate a quotient of two functions.

title: Apply the chain rule
blurb: Apply the chain rule to differentiate composite functions.

title: Combine the differentiation rules
blurb: Identify and apply the appropriate combination of the product, quotient and chain rules.

title: Find where a function increases or decreases
blurb: Use the sign of f'(c) to determine where a function is increasing or decreasing.

title: Find stationary points
blurb: Find stationary points by solving $f'(x)=0$.

title: Sketch the derivative function
blurb: Sketch $y=f'(x)$ for a given graph of $y=f(x)$.

title: Analyse stationary points of a cubic
blurb: Identify stationary points and intervals of increase and decrease for a cubic by differentiating.

title: Interpret the derivative as a rate
blurb: Interpret $f'(c)$ as the instantaneous rate of change of $f$ at $x=c$.

title: Distinguish displacement and velocity
blurb: Define and distinguish between displacement and distance and between velocity and speed.

title: Find velocity from displacement
blurb: Differentiate a displacement function to find the velocity of a particle moving in a straight line.

title: Describe exponential behaviour
blurb: Describe the behaviour of $y=k(a^{x})$ and $y=k(a^{-x})$ as $x\to\infty$ and $x\to-\infty$.

title: Identify Euler's number e
blurb: Identify $e\approx 2.718$ as the base for which the tangent to $y=e^{x}$ at $x=0$ has gradient $1$.

title: Recognise $e^{x}$ is its own derivative
blurb: Conclude that $\dfrac{d}{dx}(e^{x})=e^{x}$, that is, $e^{x}$ is its own derivative.

title: Define the natural logarithm
blurb: Define $\ln a=\log_e a$ and evaluate exponential and logarithmic expressions using digital tools.

title: Use the inverse log identities
blurb: Use $\log_a(a^{x})=x$ and $a^{\log_a x}=x$ to simplify expressions and solve equations.

title: Reflect graphs in the axes
blurb: Determine the effect of replacing $x$ by $-x$ and $y$ by $-y$ as reflections in the $y$- and $x$-axes.

title: Translate graphs
blurb: Determine horizontal and vertical translations from replacing $x$ by $x-a$ and $y$ by $y-b$.

title: Dilate graphs
blurb: Determine horizontal and vertical dilations and enlargements from replacing $x$ by $x/k$ and $y$ by $y/l$.

title: Apply combined transformations
blurb: Apply reflections, translations and dilations to a function and determine its rule, graph, domain, range and features.

title: Apply transformations in the correct order
blurb: Recognise and apply the importance of the order in which transformations are applied.

title: Describe transformations from an equation
blurb: Given a transformed function rule, rewrite it as $\frac{y-c}{k}=f\left(a(x-b)\right)$ and describe the sequence of reflections, dilations and translations applied.

title: Use set notation
blurb: Use set notation for elements, n(A) or |A|, the empty set, subsets, intersection, union and disjoint sets.

title: Apply the inclusion–exclusion rule
blurb: Apply $|A\cup B|=|A|+|B|-|A\cap B|$ using Venn diagrams.

title: Apply the complement and addition rules
blurb: Apply $P(\bar{A})=1-P(A)$ and $P(A\cup B)=P(A)+P(B)-P(A\cap B)$.

title: Use arrays and tree diagrams
blurb: Use arrays and tree diagrams to determine the outcomes and probabilities for multistage events.

title: Test events for independence
blurb: Test whether two events are independent using $P(A|B)=P(A)$ and $P(A\cap B)=P(A)\times P(B)$.

title: Reverse conditional probability with trees
blurb: Find a reverse conditional probability $P(B|A)$ for multistage events by building a tree from $P(B)$, $P(A|B)$ and $P(A|\bar{B})$, then applying $P(B|A)=\dfrac{P(A\cap B)}{P(A\cap B)+P(\bar{B}\cap A)}$.

title: Distinguish discrete and continuous variables
blurb: Define a random variable and distinguish discrete from continuous random variables with examples.

title: Organise data in frequency tables
blurb: Organise data into frequency, relative frequency and cumulative frequency tables.

title: Graph frequency and cumulative frequency
blurb: Graph frequency, relative frequency and cumulative frequency histograms and polygons and identify the mode and median.

title: Estimate probability from relative frequency
blurb: Use relative frequency to estimate the probability of results in experiments.

title: Transform trigonometric graphs
blurb: Graph transformations of $y=\sin x$, $y=\cos x$ and $y=\tan x$ and determine amplitude, period, translations, domain and range.

title: Solve transformed trig equations
blurb: Solve equations and graphical problems involving transformed trigonometric functions within a specified domain.

title: Model periodic phenomena
blurb: Model and solve problems involving periodic phenomena using transformed trigonometric functions, without calculus.

title: Model with function transformations
blurb: Model and solve practical problems using functions and their transformations.

title: Apply logarithmic scales
blurb: Use logarithmic scales to model and solve problems involving decibels, seismic scales, star magnitudes and pH.

title: Solve trig equations graphically
blurb: Solve or count the solutions of a trigonometric equation by sketching each side, and use symmetry and the period to find every solution in a domain from one known solution.

title: Find a sinusoidal model from features
blurb: Determine $k$, $a$ and $c$ for a model $y=k\cos(at)+c$ (or $\sin$) from a described maximum, minimum and period, using amplitude $=\frac{\max-\min}{2}$ and mean position $=\frac{\max+\min}{2}$.

title: Use sequence notation
blurb: Use $a_{n}$ for the $n$th term and distinguish finite from infinite sequences.

title: Define partial sums and series
blurb: Define the $n$th partial sum $S_{n}$ and a series as the sum of the terms of a sequence.

title: Use summation (sigma) notation
blurb: Use summation notation to represent and evaluate the sum of terms of a sequence.

title: Find the nth term of an AP
blurb: Identify an arithmetic sequence and use $a_{n}=a+(n-1)d$ to solve problems.

title: Find the sum of an AP
blurb: Use $S_{n}=\dfrac{n}{2}(a+a_{n})$ and $S_{n}=\dfrac{n}{2}[2a+(n-1)d]$ to find the partial sum of an arithmetic sequence.

title: Find the number of terms of an arithmetic series
blurb: Form and solve a quadratic in $n$ from $S_n=\dfrac{n}{2}[2a+(n-1)d]$ to find the number of terms, rejecting non-positive or non-integer solutions.

title: Model linear growth and decay with APs
blurb: Apply arithmetic sequences and their partial sums to model linear growth and decay problems.

title: Find the nth term of a GP
blurb: Identify a geometric sequence and use $a_{n}=ar^{n-1}$ to solve problems.

title: Find the sum of a GP
blurb: Use $S_{n}=\dfrac{a(1-r^{n})}{1-r}$ to find the sum of the first $n$ terms of a geometric sequence.

title: Find the limiting sum of a GP
blurb: Find the limiting sum $S_\infty=\dfrac{a}{1-r}$ of a geometric series with $|r|<1$.

title: Model growth and decay with GPs
blurb: Apply geometric sequences and series to model exponential growth and decay problems.

title: Differentiate exponential functions
blurb: Differentiate $f(x)=ke^{ax}$ and use the chain rule to show $\dfrac{d}{dx}(e^{ax+b})=ae^{ax+b}$.

title: Differentiate $a^{x}$
blurb: Prove and use $\dfrac{d}{dx}(a^{x})=(\ln a)a^{x}$.

title: Differentiate $e^{f(x)}$
blurb: Use the chain rule to differentiate functions of the form $e^{f(x)}$.

title: Differentiate ln x
blurb: Prove and use $\dfrac{d}{dx}(\ln x)=\dfrac{1}{x}$ for $x>0$.

title: Differentiate $\log_a x$
blurb: Prove and use $\dfrac{d}{dx}(\log_a x)=\dfrac{1}{x\ln a}$.

title: Differentiate ln f(x)
blurb: Use the chain rule to differentiate functions of the form ln f(x).

title: Differentiate sin and cos
blurb: Use $\dfrac{d}{dx}(\sin x)=\cos x$ and $\dfrac{d}{dx}(\cos x)=-\sin x$.

title: Differentiate tan, sec, cosec and cot
blurb: Differentiate tan x, sec x, cosec x and cot x using the rules of differentiation.

title: Differentiate composite trig functions
blurb: Use the chain rule to differentiate sin f(x), cos f(x) and tan f(x).

title: Differentiate combinations of functions
blurb: Apply the product, quotient and chain rules to differentiate combinations of exponential, logarithmic, trigonometric and algebraic functions.

title: Find tangents and normals for any function
blurb: Solve problems involving the equations of tangents and normals to curves involving any function in the course.

title: Define a primitive function
blurb: Define a primitive $F(x)$ where $F'(x)=f(x)$ and recognise that primitives differ by a constant $+C$.

title: Find primitives of power functions
blurb: Find primitives of sums of $kx^{n}$ using $F(x)=\dfrac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}$ for $n\ne -1$.

title: Find primitives of $(ax+b)^{n}$
blurb: Find the primitive of functions of the form $(ax+b)^{n}$ for real $n\ne -1$.

title: Find a function from its derivative
blurb: Determine $f(x)$ given $f'(x)$ and an initial condition $f(a)=b$.

title: Interpret the definite integral as a limit of sums
blurb: Interpret $\sum f(x)\Delta x$ and the definite integral as the limit of sums of areas.

title: Approximate area with rectangles or trapezia
blurb: Approximate the area under a curve by summing rectangles or trapezia and refine as $\Delta x\to 0$.

title: Evaluate definite integrals using areas
blurb: Evaluate definite integrals using areas from geometric formulas, including where f(x) changes sign.

title: Apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
blurb: Recognise $A(x)=\displaystyle\int_a^x f(t)\,dt$ as a primitive of $f$ and apply $\displaystyle\int_a^b f(x)\,dx=F(b)-F(a)$.

title: Use indefinite integral notation
blurb: Use $\displaystyle\int f(x)\,dx=F(x)+C$ and the sum and multiple rules for primitives.

title: Integrate power functions
blurb: Use $\displaystyle\int x^{n}\,dx=\dfrac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+C$ for real $n\ne -1$.

title: Apply the reverse chain rule
blurb: Apply $\displaystyle\int f'(x)[f(x)]^{n}\,dx=\dfrac{[f(x)]^{n+1}}{n+1}+C$ for $n\ne -1$.

title: Integrate exponential functions
blurb: Integrate $e^{x}$ and $e^{ax+b}$.

title: Integrate $a^{x}$
blurb: Use $\displaystyle\int a^{x}\,dx=\dfrac{a^{x}}{\ln a}+C$.

title: Integrate $e^{f(x)}f'(x)$
blurb: Apply $\displaystyle\int f'(x)e^{f(x)}\,dx=e^{f(x)}+C$.

title: Integrate $\dfrac{1}{x}$ and $\dfrac{1}{ax+b}$
blurb: Use $\displaystyle\int\dfrac{1}{x}\,dx=\ln|x|+C$ and $\displaystyle\int\dfrac{1}{ax+b}\,dx=\dfrac{1}{a}\ln|ax+b|+C$.

title: Integrate $\dfrac{f'(x)}{f(x)}$
blurb: Apply $\displaystyle\int\dfrac{f'(x)}{f(x)}\,dx=\ln|f(x)|+C$ on a domain where $f(x)\ne 0$.

title: Integrate $\sin$, $\cos$ and $\sec^{2}$
blurb: Use $\displaystyle\int\sin x\,dx=-\cos x+C$, $\displaystyle\int\cos x\,dx=\sin x+C$ and $\displaystyle\int\sec^{2}x\,dx=\tan x+C$.

title: Integrate trig functions of ax+b
blurb: Determine indefinite integrals of the form $\displaystyle\int f(ax+b)\,dx$ for $\sin$, $\cos$ and $\sec^{2}$.

title: Integrate composite trig forms
blurb: Determine integrals of the form $\displaystyle\int f'(x)\sin f(x)\,dx$, $\displaystyle\int f'(x)\cos f(x)\,dx$ and $\displaystyle\int f'(x)\sec^{2}f(x)\,dx$.

title: Find the area under a curve
blurb: Calculate the area between a curve and the x-axis, accounting for regions below the axis.

title: Find the area between a curve and the y-axis
blurb: Calculate the area between a curve and the y-axis between two values of y.

title: Use symmetry in integration
blurb: Use symmetry (odd and even functions) and properties of definite integrals to simplify area calculations.

title: Approximate integrals with the Trapezoidal rule
blurb: Use the Trapezoidal rule to approximate a definite integral.

title: Solve practical area problems
blurb: Model and solve practical problems involving the areas of regions bounded by a curve and an axis.

title: Relate differentiability and continuity
blurb: Define differentiability, recognise that it implies continuity, and identify points that are continuous but not differentiable.

title: Find second derivatives
blurb: Use repeated differentiation to find second derivatives f''(x).

title: Classify stationary points (first-derivative test)
blurb: Classify stationary points as local maxima, local minima or horizontal points of inflection using the first-derivative test.

title: Determine concavity
blurb: Use the sign of f''(x) to determine where a curve is concave up or concave down.

title: Locate points of inflection
blurb: Define a point of inflection and locate it where the concavity changes, using the sign of f''(x).

title: Classify stationary points (second-derivative test)
blurb: Use the second derivative to classify a stationary point as a maximum, minimum or horizontal point of inflection.

title: Sketch curves using calculus
blurb: Sketch a function using stationary points, points of inflection, symmetry, domain, asymptotes and behaviour as $x\to\pm\infty$.

title: Graph the first and second derivatives
blurb: Graph $y=f'(x)$ and $y=f''(x)$ given only the graph of $y=f(x)$.

title: Model an optimisation problem
blurb: Model an optimisation problem by defining variables, noting domain restrictions and forming an objective function.

title: Find global maxima and minima on a domain
blurb: Find the global maximum and minimum of a function on a closed domain by comparing its values at stationary points, domain endpoints and any discontinuities.

title: Solve optimisation problems
blurb: Solve optimisation problems by modelling the objective function, finding and classifying its stationary points, and interpreting the solution in context.

title: Interpret rates of change with derivatives
blurb: Use the first and second derivatives to find and interpret rates of change where a quantity is a function of time.

title: Solve rate-of-change problems by integration
blurb: Use integration with initial conditions to solve problems where a rate of change is given as a function of time.

title: Recognise $\dfrac{dQ}{dt}=kQ$

title: Dot products of unit vectors
blurb: Establish and use $i \cdot i=j \cdot j=k \cdot k=1$ and $i \cdot j=j \cdot k=k \cdot i=0$.

title: Prove the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality
blurb: Prove and use the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality $|a \cdot b| \le |a||b|$ for vectors.

title: Triangle centres in vectors
blurb: Define the medians, altitudes, perpendicular bisectors and angle bisectors of a triangle and recognise these in vector proofs.

title: Prove geometric results with vectors
blurb: Solve problems and prove geometric results in 2D and 3D using vectors.

title: Define the imaginary unit
blurb: Define the number $i$ by $i^{2}=-1$.

title: Solve $x^{2}+k=0$
blurb: Use the number $i$ to solve quadratic equations of the form $x^{2}+k=0$ where $k$ is a positive real number.

title: Define the complex numbers
blurb: Define the complex numbers ℂ as the set of numbers of the form a+ib where a and b are real.

title: Complex roots of a quadratic
blurb: Express the roots of a real-coefficient quadratic with negative discriminant as complex numbers.

title: Real and imaginary parts
blurb: Identify the real part $\text{Re}(z)$ and imaginary part $\text{Im}(z)$ of a complex number $z=a+ib$.

title: Classify numbers by set
blurb: Classify numbers as natural numbers, integers, rationals, reals or complex numbers, each an extension of the previous.

title: Equality of complex numbers
blurb: Use the condition that $z_{1}=z_{2}$ if and only if their real parts are equal and their imaginary parts are equal.

title: Add, subtract and multiply complex numbers
blurb: Perform addition, subtraction and multiplication of complex numbers.

title: Use the complex conjugate
blurb: Define the complex conjugate $\bar{z}=a-ib$ of $z=a+ib$ and use it to solve problems.

title: Modulus of a complex number
blurb: Define and calculate the modulus $|z|=\sqrt{z\bar{z}}=\sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}$.

title: Relationships with conjugate and modulus
blurb: Establish and use $\text{Re}(z)=\dfrac{z+\bar{z}}{2}$, $\text{Im}(z)=\dfrac{z-\bar{z}}{2i}$ and $\dfrac{1}{z}=\dfrac{\bar{z}}{|z|^{2}}$.

title: Divide complex numbers
blurb: Divide one complex number by another non-zero complex number and give the result in the form a+ib.

title: Square roots of a complex number
blurb: Find the two square roots of a complex number $z=a+ib$.

title: Plot on the complex plane
blurb: Plot a complex number $z=a+ib$ as a point on the complex (Argand) plane.

title: Argument of a complex number
blurb: Define and calculate the argument $\theta$ of a non-zero complex number, noting it has multiple values differing by multiples of $2\pi$.

title: Principal argument
blurb: Define and use the principal argument $\text{Arg}(z)$, the unique argument in the interval $(-\pi,\pi]$.

title: Polar form of a complex number
blurb: Express a complex number in modulus–argument form $z=r(\cos\theta+i\sin\theta)$ and represent it on the complex plane.

title: Multiply and divide in polar form
blurb: Multiply, divide and take powers of complex numbers in polar form and interpret these geometrically.

title: Convert between Cartesian and polar form
blurb: Convert between Cartesian and polar form and use both to solve problems.

title: Prove modulus identities
blurb: Prove and use $|z_{1}z_{2}|=|z_{1}||z_{2}|$, $\left|\dfrac{z_{1}}{z_{2}}\right|=\dfrac{|z_{1}|}{|z_{2}|}$ and $|z^{n}|=|z|^{n}$ for integer $n$.

title: Prove argument identities
blurb: Prove and use $\arg(z_{1}z_{2})=\arg z_{1}+\arg z_{2}$, $\arg\left(\dfrac{z_{1}}{z_{2}}\right)=\arg z_{1}-\arg z_{2}$ and $\arg(z^{n})=n \cdot \arg z$.

title: Prove conjugate identities
blurb: Prove and use conjugate identities for sums and products of complex numbers.

title: Triangle inequality for complex numbers
blurb: Prove and use the triangle inequality $|z_{1}+z_{2}| \le |z_{1}|+|z_{2}|$ for complex numbers.

title: Solve quadratics with complex coefficients
blurb: Solve quadratic equations $ax^{2}+bx+c=0$ where $a$, $b$ and $c$ are complex numbers.

title: Conjugate roots of real quadratics
blurb: Recognise that solutions of real-coefficient quadratics are complex conjugates and use this to solve problems.

title: Prove the conjugate root theorem
blurb: Prove the complex conjugate root theorem: if z is a root of a real-coefficient polynomial then z̅ is also a root.

title: Solve polynomial conjugate-root problems
blurb: Solve problems involving complex conjugate roots of polynomial equations with real coefficients.

title: Prove de Moivre’s theorem (positive n)
blurb: Prove de Moivre’s theorem $(\cos\theta+i\sin\theta)^{n}=\cos n\theta+i\sin n\theta$ for positive integer $n$ using mathematical induction.

title: de Moivre’s theorem for negative n
blurb: Prove de Moivre’s theorem holds for negative integer powers.

title: Find powers with de Moivre’s theorem
blurb: Use de Moivre’s theorem to find any integer power of a given complex number.

title: Derive trig identities with de Moivre
blurb: Use de Moivre’s theorem to derive trigonometric identities.

title: nth roots of $\pm 1$
blurb: Determine the nth roots of $\pm 1$ in polar form and their location on the unit circle.

title: Geometry of the nth roots of $\pm 1$
blurb: Illustrate the geometric relationship connecting the nth roots of $\pm 1$.

title: nth roots of a complex number
blurb: Determine the nth roots of a complex number and their location on the complex plane.

title: Represent a complex number as a vector
blurb: Recognise a complex number as a vector whose magnitude and direction are its modulus and argument.

title: Add and subtract complex numbers as vectors
blurb: Examine and use addition and subtraction of complex numbers as vectors on the complex plane.

title: Geometry of complex multiplication
blurb: Interpret multiplication of complex numbers geometrically as rotation and dilation on the complex plane.

title: Prove results with complex numbers as vectors
blurb: Prove geometric results using complex numbers as vectors.

title: Solve problems with nth roots
blurb: Solve problems and prove results using the nth roots of complex numbers.

title: Graph $\text{Re}(z)=c$ and $\text{Im}(z)=k$
blurb: Graph vertical and horizontal lines of the form $\text{Re}(z)=c$ or $\text{Im}(z)=k$.

title: Graph $|z-z_{1}|=|z-z_{2}|$
blurb: Graph the line $|z-z_{1}|=|z-z_{2}|$ and give a geometrical description of it.

title: Graph $|z|=r$ and $|z-z_{1}|=r$
blurb: Graph the circles $|z|=r$ and $|z-z_{1}|=r$.

title: Graph rays $\arg(z)=\theta$
blurb: Graph rays corresponding to $\arg(z)=\theta$ and $\arg(z-z_{1})=\theta$.

title: Graph regions on the complex plane
blurb: Graph regions associated with lines, rays and circles defined using complex numbers, using circle geometry theorems where necessary.

title: Derive product-to-sum identities
blurb: Derive the identities expressing cosAcosB, sinAsinB, sinAcosB and cosAsinB as sums and differences.

title: Apply product-to-sum identities
blurb: Use the product-to-sum identities to solve problems and prove results.

title: Solve trig equations with product-to-sum
blurb: Solve trigonometric equations using the product-to-sum formulas over restricted domains.

title: Integrate products of sine and cosine
blurb: Use product-to-sum identities to evaluate integrals of the form $\int \sin(mx)\cos(nx)\, dx$, $\int \sin(mx)\sin(nx)\, dx$ or $\int \cos(mx)\cos(nx)\, dx$.

title: Derive the t-formulas
blurb: Derive $\sin A=\dfrac{2t}{1+t^{2}}$, $\cos A=\dfrac{1-t^{2}}{1+t^{2}}$ and $\tan A=\dfrac{2t}{1-t^{2}}$ where $t=\tan\dfrac{A}{2}$.

title: Solve trig equations with the t-formulas
blurb: Use the t-formulas to solve trigonometric equations over restricted domains.

title: Decompose into partial fractions
blurb: Decompose rational functions whose denominators are products of distinct linear, distinct irreducible quadratic and perfect-square factors into partial fractions.

title: Integrate using partial fractions
blurb: Integrate rational functions using partial fraction decomposition.

title: Integrate by completing the square
blurb: Integrate rational functions by completing the square on a quadratic denominator.

title: Integrate improper rational functions
blurb: Integrate rational functions where the degree of the numerator is not less than the degree of the denominator.

title: Integrate by algebraic manipulation
blurb: Integrate functions by changing the integrand into an appropriate form using algebraic manipulation.

title: Derive integration by parts
blurb: Derive the method of integration by parts from the product rule.

title: Integrate by parts
blurb: Find indefinite and evaluate definite integrals using integration by parts, including problems requiring more than one application.

title: Recurrence relations with integration by parts
blurb: Derive and use recurrence relations involving integration by parts.

title: Combine integration techniques (theory)
blurb: Solve theoretical problems involving multiple techniques of integration.

title: Combine integration techniques (practical)
blurb: Solve practical problems involving multiple techniques of integration.

title: Express acceleration three ways
blurb: Derive the expressions for acceleration $\dfrac{dv}{dt}$, $v\dfrac{dv}{dx}$ and $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left(\tfrac{1}{2}v^{2}\right)$.

title: Motion with velocity-dependent acceleration
blurb: Solve problems with velocity and acceleration expressed in terms of displacement, and acceleration in terms of velocity.

title: Newton’s laws of motion
blurb: Examine Newton’s three laws of motion, including force, acceleration, action and reaction under constant and non-constant force.

title: Use $F=m\ddot{x}$
blurb: Find acceleration $\ddot{x}$ using $F=m\ddot{x}$, where $F$ is the force acting on a mass $m$.

title: Resolve forces into components
blurb: Recognise forces as vectors, resolve concurrent forces into perpendicular components and use projections to find the force in a given direction in 2D and 3D.

title: Define simple harmonic motion
blurb: Define simple harmonic motion as $\ddot{x}=-n^{2}(x-c)$, where acceleration is proportional to and opposite in direction to displacement from the centre.

title: Restoring force in SHM
blurb: Recognise the force in SHM as a restoring force acting towards the centre of motion.

title: Verify SHM solutions
blurb: Verify that $x=A\sin(nt+\alpha)+c$, $x=A\cos(nt+\alpha)+c$ and combinations of these satisfy the SHM differential equation.

title: Describe SHM
blurb: Describe simple harmonic motion using displacement, velocity, acceleration, force, amplitude and period.

title: Prove motion is SHM
blurb: Prove that motion is simple harmonic by obtaining $\ddot{x}=-n^{2}(x-c)$ from a given equation for acceleration, velocity or displacement.

title: Graph SHM
blurb: Graph displacement, velocity and acceleration as functions of time for a particle in simple harmonic motion.

title: SHM equations from graphs
blurb: Determine equations for simple harmonic motion given graphs of acceleration, velocity or displacement against time.

title: Derive the SHM velocity equation
blurb: Derive $v^{2}=n^{2}[A^{2}-(x-c)^{2}]$ for a particle in simple harmonic motion, where $A$ is the amplitude.

title: SHM displacement and velocity in time
blurb: Determine displacement and velocity in terms of time for an object in SHM satisfying given initial conditions.

title: Solve SHM problems
blurb: Model and solve problems involving simple harmonic motion using relevant formulas and graphs.

title: Equations of motion without resistance
blurb: Derive and use the equations of motion for a particle moving in a straight line with constant and variable acceleration and no resistance.

title: Motion on a smooth inclined plane
blurb: Solve problems on a smooth inclined plane by resolving forces into components parallel and perpendicular to the plane.

title: Pulley and inclined-plane problems
blurb: Solve motion problems involving a single smooth pulley and a smooth inclined plane.

title: Acceleration in resisted motion
blurb: Derive, from $\Sigma F=m\ddot{x}$, the acceleration equation for a particle resisted by a force proportional to a power of the speed.

title: Resisted velocity as a function of time
blurb: Derive an expression for velocity as a function of time for a particle in rectilinear resisted motion.

title: Resisted velocity as a function of displacement
blurb: Derive an expression for velocity as a function of displacement for a particle in rectilinear resisted motion.

title: Resisted displacement as a function of time
blurb: Derive an expression for displacement as a function of time for a particle in rectilinear resisted motion.

title: Solve rectilinear resisted-motion problems
blurb: Solve problems, excluding pulley systems, involving a particle resisted by a force proportional to a power of the speed.

title: Acceleration in vertical resisted motion
blurb: Derive the acceleration equation for a particle moving vertically under gravity with resistance proportional to the first or second power of the speed.

title: Velocity in vertical resisted motion
blurb: Derive velocity as a function of time and of displacement for vertical resisted motion under gravity.

title: Displacement in vertical resisted motion
blurb: Derive displacement as a function of time for vertical resisted motion under gravity.

title: Define terminal velocity
blurb: Define the terminal velocity of a particle falling through a medium as the constant velocity reached when resistance prevents further acceleration.

title: Determine terminal velocity
blurb: Determine the terminal velocity of a falling particle from its equation of motion for vertical resisted motion.

title: Solve vertical resisted-motion problems
blurb: Solve vertical resisted-motion problems, including maximum height, times of flight and terminal velocity.

title: Trajectory shapes with resistance
blurb: Distinguish the trajectory of a projectile with negligible resistance from that of a projectile subject to resistance proportional to the speed.

title: Equations of resisted projectile motion
blurb: Establish and use the acceleration equations for a projectile launched at an angle and subject to resistance proportional to the speed.
